commands.purpose: List roles filtered by tenant / application / guard. Without --include-system, custom roles only. Support-facing diagnostic — no state mutation.

Print one role's full detail: name, guard, application, tenant, is_system, description, sort_order, permission count. --permissions expands the permission list; --principals lists every principal currently holding the role.

Re-seed default system roles for a specific tenant. Reads role_definitions rows matching the tenant's business_type + min_tier gate, UPSERTs the roles + attaches default_permissions. Idempotent. --dry-run reports intended changes without writing. --force overrides the safety refusal that fires when the tenant already has custom roles with the same name.

Reconcile role_definitions rows with data/role-catalogue.json + the permission catalogue. Called by the release pipeline after every deploy. --refresh-catalogue also re-loads permissions.json blueprints from every #[ProvidesPermissions] provider (invalidates rbac:permissions:catalogue:{application_id}).
List roles held by a principal (User / PlatformUser / ServiceAccount). --include-expired shows past assignments (expires_at < now()).
Support-facing role assignment. --reason required in production (rbac.commands.require_reason=true). Enforces the same GuardBoundaryEnforcer + ApplicationMismatch checks as the HTTP path.
Support-facing role revocation. Fires RoleRevokedFromPrincipal + invalidates the principal's permission cache.
Filter permissions by role / principal / module owner / name pattern (glob-like). Useful for auditing 'what does permission `athletes.viewAny.branch` currently attach to?'.

Trace how a permission resolves for a principal. Prints the full 5-step algorithm output: which grants applied (deny/allow), which roles matched, whether delegation fired, final outcome. --verbose also prints the resolved permission set + cache key. The single most useful diagnostic for 'why can Alice see this invoice?' support tickets.
Manual trigger for the hourly PurgeExpiredRoleAssignmentsJob. --dry-run reports rows that would be purged.
One-shot migration helper for tenants upgrading from earlier RBAC schemas (pre-Wave 1b). --map=/path/to/role-map.json takes { "old_role_name": "new_role_name", ... }. Idempotent.

safety.require_reason: rbac:assign-role and rbac:revoke-role refuse to run without --reason=<text> when rbac.commands.require_reason=true (default true in staging + prod). The reason is written to the audit log alongside the mutation.
